title: identity — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by IdentityServiceProvider::boot() so any FormRequest / Data class can validate credential-related input. All four rules operate on scalar input (strings, dates).

rules.name: strong_password
rules.description: Enforces every `identity.password.*` config key (min length, complexity, breach check). Composed of sub-rules that fire independently so the error message names which requirement failed (e.g. `Password must be at least 12 characters`, `Password must contain at least one uppercase letter`, `Password has been found in 342 public data breaches`). When `identity.password.breach_check_enabled=true`, submits the SHA-1 prefix (first 5 hex chars) to haveibeenpwned's k-anonymity API and matches the remainder client-side — the plaintext NEVER leaves the app.
rules.default_message: Password does not meet security requirements. :reason

rules.name: bcp47_locale
rules.description: Validates a BCP-47 language tag (e.g. `en`, `fr-CA`, `zh-Hans-HK`). Uses the same regex as `platform_languages.bcp47_code`. Used on `dob` locale disambiguation and on Profile.locale in the referring `user` module.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid BCP-47 language tag (e.g. `en`, `fr-CA`, `zh-Hans`).

rules.name: valid_recovery_code
rules.description: Validates a submitted MFA recovery code against the bcrypt hashes stored in Identity.mfa_recovery_codes_hashed. Consumes the code atomically — removes it from the array so a replay is refused on second use. Constant-time hash comparison to prevent timing attacks.

params.name: identity_id
params.description: The Identity whose recovery codes should be checked. Passed as an argument at validation time from the request context (never trusted from user input).

rules.default_message: The recovery code is invalid or has already been used.

rules.name: not_in_password_history
rules.description: Rejects the new password when its bcrypt/argon2id hash matches any entry in Identity.password_history_hashed. History window size configured by `identity.password.history_size` (default 5).

params.name: identity_id
params.description: The Identity whose password history should be checked.

rules.default_message: You cannot reuse any of your last :history_size passwords.

usage_from_data_class: Data classes composed with `RegisterIdentityInput`, `ChangePasswordInput`, `ResetPasswordInput` automatically wire the rules via #[Validation] attribute on each property; consumers rarely reference the rules by name.
